properties.name: Century Sebree Smelter
properties.notes: Largest operating US smelter. Ohio River barge access. Owned by Century since 2003.

properties.name: Century Mt. Holly Smelter
properties.capacity_notes: Nameplate 229kt; operating at ~75% (172kt in 2023); $50M restart to full capacity by June 2026
properties.notes: Only US smelter producing high-purity (99.9%) aluminum for military/aerospace. 2 potlines, 360 cells, Alcoa A697 tech at 228kA. Santee Cooper 3yr power contract through 2026. Century announced $50M to restore full capacity by mid-2026.

properties.name: Century Hawesville Smelter
properties.notes: 5 potlines, 560 cells, Kaiser P69 tech at 170kA. Ohio River adjacent. Idled since 2022 due to high power costs. Restart depends on competitive long-term energy contract.

properties.name: Alcoa Warrick Smelter
properties.capacity_notes: 3 operating potlines; up to 156kt actual production
properties.products: primary ingot, molten metal (to adjacent Kaiser rolling mill)
properties.notes: Co-located with Kaiser Warrick rolling mill (sold to Kaiser 2021). Alcoa retained smelter + 800MW coal power plant. 3 potlines of 150 pots each. Supplies molten metal directly to Kaiser casthouse.

properties.name: Alcoa Massena Smelter
properties.notes: Oldest continuously operating aluminum smelter site in US. St. Lawrence River hydropower. Massena East plant uses older Söderberg technology. 3yr labor agreement ratified 2023.

properties.name: Magnitude 7 New Madrid Smelter
properties.notes: Mississippi River barge access. Originally Noranda, acquired by ARG International 2016. Full shutdown Jan 2024 with no scheduled restart. Previously produced ~250kt/yr.

properties.name: Century Green Aluminum Smelter
properties.notes: First new US smelter in ~45 years. DOE Office of Clean Energy Demonstrations $500M grant. 100% renewable/nuclear power target. 75% less carbon-intensive. Site selection Feb 2026 (Inola, OK). 5-6yr build timeline.

properties.name: Novelis Oswego
properties.capacity_notes: Largest Novelis NA plant; ~360kt/yr; hot mill fire Sep 2025, restarted Dec 2025
properties.products: can stock, auto body sheet, industrial sheet
properties.notes: Novelis's largest wholly-owned NA fabrication facility. Full scrap remelting/recycling, ingot casting, hot and cold rolling, finishing. Major fire Sep 2025 damaged hot mill; restarted Dec 2025. Lake Ontario water access.

properties.name: Novelis Bay Minette
properties.capacity_notes: Initial 600kt finished goods; expandable to 1,000kt (Danieli 1+4 HSM)
properties.notes: $2.5B investment. First fully integrated aluminum mill built in US in 40+ years. South Alabama Mega Site at I-65/Hwy 287. 51km from Port of Mobile. Danieli 1+4 HSM. Commissioning H2 2026. UBC recycling center adds 15B cans/yr capacity. Railroad-served for low-carbon logistics.

properties.name: Novelis Terre Haute (Logan Aluminum)
properties.notes: Logan Aluminum joint venture. Major can stock producer supplying Ball, Crown, Ardagh. Large UBC recycling operation.

properties.name: Novelis Berea
properties.notes: Cold rolling and finishing operations. Automotive sheet focus.

properties.name: Novelis Greensboro
properties.notes: Aluminum recycling facility. $36M expansion 2019. UBC processing for can stock supply chain.

properties.name: Novelis Guthrie (formerly Aleris)
properties.notes: Acquired via Aleris acquisition 2020. Automotive sheet finishing. $500M+ expansion ongoing.

properties.name: Arconic Davenport Works
properties.capacity_notes: One of world's largest aluminum rolling mills
properties.notes: Flagship Arconic rolling mill. Mississippi River barge access. Produces aerospace plate (2xxx, 7xxx), automotive sheet, and industrial products. One of the largest aluminum rolling complexes in the world.

properties.name: Arconic Tennessee Operations
properties.notes: Originally Alcoa's historic Tennessee Works. Now Arconic rolled products. Major automotive sheet producer. $100M+ hot mill expansion completed ~2020. City of Alcoa named after the company.

properties.name: Arconic Lancaster
properties.notes: Cold rolling, finishing, and heat treatment for aerospace and industrial applications.

properties.name: Arconic Danville
properties.notes: Cold rolling and finishing operations.

properties.name: Arconic Hutchinson Aerospace Center
properties.notes: Aerospace-focused plate and sheet finishing center.

properties.name: Constellium Muscle Shoals
properties.capacity_notes: >1B lbs/yr finished coils; widest strip mill in US; 340kt recycling capacity
properties.products: can stock (body, end, tab), auto body sheet
properties.notes: 100+ acres under roof. 5 casting units. Widest strip mill in US. Element 13 recycling center processes 20B+ cans/yr. DoD $23M DPA grant for new DC casting center adding 136kt (300M lbs) annual casting capacity. $65M total expansion. Formerly Wise Metals/Wise Alloys.

properties.name: Constellium Ravenswood
properties.notes: One of world's largest rolled products facilities. World's most powerful plate stretchers. Ohio River barge access. DOE $75M grant for low/zero-carbon casthouse using clean hydrogen. Major defense/aerospace plate supplier.

properties.name: Constellium Bowling Green
properties.products: automotive structural parts, crash management systems
properties.notes: Automotive Structures & Industry division. Produces structural parts and crash management systems for OEMs.

properties.name: Kaiser Warrick Rolling Mill
properties.capacity_notes: Cast house, hot mill, cold mills, finishing/coating lines, slitters
properties.notes: Acquired from Alcoa 2021. Co-located with Alcoa smelter which supplies molten metal. $150M roll coating line addition (operational 2024). Major can stock producer.

properties.name: Kaiser Trentwood Rolling Mill
properties.capacity_notes: Plate and sheet for aerospace/general engineering
properties.notes: Historic Kaiser aluminum plant. Major aerospace plate producer including KaiserSelect AA7099. Originally government war plant, purchased by Kaiser 1946.

properties.name: JW Aluminum Russellville
properties.notes: Continuous cast and cold rolling. Building products focus.

properties.name: JW Aluminum Mount Holly
properties.notes: Continuous cast sheet production for building products.

properties.name: JW Aluminum St. Louis
properties.notes: Foil and light-gauge sheet production.

properties.name: Golden Aluminum
properties.notes: Continuous cast can stock producer. DOE $22.3M grant to upgrade casting and rolling equipment. Scrap-based production.

properties.name: Granges Huntingdon
properties.products: clad sheet, brazing sheet, fin stock
properties.notes: Formerly Noranda rolled products. Acquired by Granges 2016. Specialty clad/brazing sheet for heat exchangers.

properties.name: Granges Newport
properties.notes: Formerly Noranda. Thin-gauge rolling for HVAC applications.

properties.name: Granges Salisbury
properties.notes: Formerly Noranda. Strip and sheet products.

properties.name: Tri-Arrows Aluminum Louisville
properties.notes: Formerly ARCO Aluminum/IMCO Recycling. JV of Japanese interests. Ohio River barge access. Major UBC recycling and can stock production.

properties.name: Aluminum Dynamics Columbus
properties.products: can stock, auto body sheet, common alloy sheet
properties.notes: SDI's first aluminum mill. $1.9B+. Adjacent to SDI Columbus steel mill. SMS 1+4 hot strip mill, 2 tandem cold mills, CASH lines, coating. Tenn-Tom Waterway barge access. Class I railroad. 2 satellite UBC recycling/casting facilities (AZ and San Luis Potosi MX). Construction started Jul 2023; operations mid-2025.

properties.name: Novelis Lewisport (formerly Aleris)
properties.notes: Formerly Aleris, acquired by Novelis 2020, then divested to American Industrial Partners per DOJ requirement. Ohio River access. Automotive body sheet.

properties.name: Aleris Uhrichsville
properties.notes: Continuous cast sheet producer. Industrial and building product focus.

properties.name: Aleris Richmond
properties.notes: Formerly Aleris rolled products. Sheet finishing.

properties.name: Commonwealth Rolled Products Lewisport
properties.notes: Ohio River access. Can stock and common alloy sheet production.

properties.name: Matalco Lordstown
properties.notes: North America's largest independent billet remelter. Wagstaff Air-Slip casting. 75% scrap / 25% prime ratio. Triple M Metal scrap processing on-site. Billet 7-16 inch diameter.

properties.name: Matalco Canton
properties.notes: Acquired as Thakar Aluminum 2010. 7-12 inch billet. Upgraded systems 2011.

properties.name: Matalco Bluffton
properties.notes: 95,000 sq ft facility. Scrap-based billet production.

properties.name: Matalco Wisconsin Rapids
properties.products: extrusion billet, rolling ingot, RSI sows
properties.notes: $80M greenfield. 110,000 sq ft. Started Nov 2020. Wagstaff ShurCast DC casting. Billet + rolling slab capability. Serves upper Midwest extruders.

properties.name: Matalco Shelbyville
properties.notes: 140,000 sq ft. Acquired Ohio Valley Aluminum. 2 melting furnaces, 4 batch homogenization furnaces.

properties.name: Matalco Franklin
properties.notes: $53.5M greenfield. 461,000 sq ft. Matalco Kentucky LLC. Billet and slab from recycled aluminum.

properties.name: Matalco Brampton
properties.notes: Matalco headquarters and original plant. Triple M Metal scrap processing adjacent.

properties.name: Real Alloy Elyria
properties.notes: Major secondary smelter. Produces specification alloy ingot for die casters and foundries.

properties.name: Real Alloy Wabash
properties.notes: DOE $67.3M grant for low-waste recycling facility in Wabash.

properties.name: Spectro Alloys Rosemount
properties.capacity_notes: Acquired by UAE's EGA affiliate Sep 2024 (110kt/yr)
properties.notes: Acquired by UAE-based aluminum producer (EGA affiliate) Sep 2024. 110kt/yr secondary production. Major Midwest secondary smelter.

properties.name: Jupiter Aluminum Hammond
properties.notes: Major secondary smelter in NW Indiana industrial corridor.

properties.name: Pennex Aluminum Wellsville
properties.notes: Billet casting and secondary smelting. Serves East Coast extruders.

properties.name: Owl's Head Alloys Bowling Green
properties.notes: Expanding operations in Bowling Green.

properties.name: Audubon Metals Henderson
properties.notes: Ohio River access. Produces specification alloys for automotive die casting.

properties.name: Beck Aluminum Lebanon
properties.notes: Acquired by Real Alloy 2016.
